title: Three Apples Fell from the Sky
author: Narine Abgaryan

setting.note: A remote mountain village called Maran, clinging to a high ridge in the Armenian highlands, where a few dozen elderly villagers outlive famine, earthquake and war and a late love still finds room to grow. Maran is invented, but it is drawn from the author's own home town of Berd in the Tavush region of north-eastern Armenia, so the map anchors the story in the real Tavush highlands rather than a fictional coordinate.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Narine Abgaryan, first published in Russian in 2015. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.translation: English translation by Lisa C. Hayden (Oneworld, 2020). None of it is stored or reproduced here; the map cites places and scenes only.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.

palette.note: The rust-red of highland earth and old tile roofs above the Tavush valley. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
